revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{神社 |名称=大鷲神社 |画像= |所在地=[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]]安食3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|...'
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=大鷲神社 |画像= |所在地=[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]]安食3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} |祭神=[[天日鷲神|天乃日鷲命]]・[[大国主|大巳貴命]]・[[スクナビコナ|小名彦命]]・[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] |社格=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] |例祭= |神事= }} '''大鷲神社'''（おおわしじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]]安食（[[下総国]][[下埴生郡]]）にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度#無格社|無格社]]。
[[天日鷲神|天乃日鷲命]]、[[大国主|大巳貴命]]、[[スクナビコナ|小名彦命]]、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]を祀る。
== 由緒 == 創祀の年代は明らかではないが、日本武尊が東夷征討の際、[[錦旗]]を立て仮の御野立所とし租神を奉斉した旧跡という。現存する本殿は凝宝珠金具の刻銘により[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）の造営とされ、栄町の[[有形文化財]]に指定されている。
[[江戸時代]]には[[徳川家光]]の[[乳母]][[春日局]]の崇敬を受けた。家光がまだ竹千代といっていたころ当社に竹千代の出世を祈願し、三代[[征夷大将軍|将軍]]になると報恩のため、将軍の[[御座船]][[天地丸]]の金色の鷲をもらいうけ献納した。古くから出世開運の神として尊崇を集めており、毎年12月の初酉の日から3日間の[[酉の市]]には福を願う多くの人々が訪れ大変な賑わいを見せる。
== 交通 == * JR[[成田線]][[安食駅]]徒歩10分
== 参考文献 == * 『ふるさとの文化遺産 郷土資料事典<12> 千葉県』人文社、1997年。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/1253/ ちばの観光まるごと紹介 大鷲神社（栄町）]
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおわししんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:栄町]] [[Category:千葉県の旧無格社]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: 画像追加
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=大鷲神社 |画像=[[File:Otori jinja sakae haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]| |所在地=[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]]安食3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} |祭神=[[天日鷲神|天乃日鷲命]]・[[大国主|大巳貴命]]・[[スクナビコナ|小名彦命]]・[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] |社格=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] |例祭= |神事= }} '''大鷲神社'''（おおわしじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]]安食（[[下総国]][[下埴生郡]]）にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度#無格社|無格社]]。
[[天日鷲神|天乃日鷲命]]、[[大国主|大巳貴命]]、[[スクナビコナ|小名彦命]]、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]を祀る。
== 由緒 == 創祀の年代は明らかではないが、日本武尊が東夷征討の際、[[錦旗]]を立て仮の御野立所とし租神を奉斉した旧跡という。現存する本殿は凝宝珠金具の刻銘により[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）の造営とされ、栄町の[[有形文化財]]に指定されている。
[[江戸時代]]には[[徳川家光]]の[[乳母]][[春日局]]の崇敬を受けた。家光がまだ竹千代といっていたころ当社に竹千代の出世を祈願し、三代[[征夷大将軍|将軍]]になると報恩のため、将軍の[[御座船]][[天地丸]]の金色の鷲をもらいうけ献納した。古くから出世開運の神として尊崇を集めており、毎年12月の初酉の日から3日間の[[酉の市]]には福を願う多くの人々が訪れ大変な賑わいを見せる。
== 交通 == * JR[[成田線]][[安食駅]]徒歩10分
== 参考文献 == * 『ふるさとの文化遺産 郷土資料事典<12> 千葉県』人文社、1997年。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/1253/ ちばの観光まるごと紹介 大鷲神社（栄町）]
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおわししんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:栄町]] [[Category:千葉県の旧無格社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称=大鷲神社 |画像=[[File:Otori jinja sakae haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]| |所在地=[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} |祭神=[[天日鷲神|天乃日鷲命]]・[[大国主|大巳貴命]]・[[スクナビコナ|小名彦命]]・[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] |社格=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] |例祭= |神事= }} '''大鷲神社'''（おおわしじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]（[[下総国]][[下埴生郡]]）にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度#無格社|無格社]]。
[[天日鷲神|天乃日鷲命]]、[[大国主|大巳貴命]]、[[スクナビコナ|小名彦命]]、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]を祀る。
== 由緒 == 創祀の年代は明らかではないが、日本武尊が東夷征討の際、[[錦旗]]を立て仮の御野立所とし租神を奉斉した旧跡という。現存する本殿は凝宝珠金具の刻銘により[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）の造営とされ、栄町の[[有形文化財]]に指定されている。
[[江戸時代]]には[[徳川家光]]の[[乳母]][[春日局]]の崇敬を受けた。家光がまだ竹千代といっていたころ当社に竹千代の出世を祈願し、三代[[征夷大将軍|将軍]]になると報恩のため、将軍の[[御座船]][[天地丸]]の金色の鷲をもらいうけ献納した。古くから出世開運の神として尊崇を集めており、毎年12月の初酉の日から3日間の[[酉の市]]には福を願う多くの人々が訪れ大変な賑わいを見せる。
== 交通 == * JR[[成田線]][[安食駅]]徒歩10分
== 参考文献 == * 『ふるさとの文化遺産 郷土資料事典<12> 千葉県』人文社、1997年。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/1253/ ちばの観光まるごと紹介 大鷲神社（栄町）]
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおわししんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:栄町]] [[Category:千葉県の旧無格社]] {{shinto-stub}}

== 外部リンク == * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/5758/ 大鷲神社／千葉県公式観光情報サイト－まるごとe! ちば－]

revision.comment: /* 外部リンク */ cat
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=大鷲神社 |画像=[[File:Otori jinja sakae haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]| |所在地=[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} |祭神=[[天日鷲神|天乃日鷲命]]・[[大国主|大巳貴命]]・[[スクナビコナ|小名彦命]]・[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] |社格=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] |例祭= |神事= }} '''大鷲神社'''（おおわしじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]（[[下総国]][[下埴生郡]]）にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度#無格社|無格社]]。
[[天日鷲神|天乃日鷲命]]、[[大国主|大巳貴命]]、[[スクナビコナ|小名彦命]]、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]を祀る。
== 由緒 == 創祀の年代は明らかではないが、日本武尊が東夷征討の際、[[錦旗]]を立て仮の[[御野立所]]とし租神を奉斉した旧跡という。現存する本殿は凝宝珠金具の刻銘により[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）の造営とされ、栄町の[[有形文化財]]に指定されている。
[[江戸時代]]には[[徳川家光]]の[[乳母]][[春日局]]の崇敬を受けた。家光がまだ竹千代といっていたころ当社に竹千代の出世を祈願し、三代[[征夷大将軍|将軍]]になると報恩のため、将軍の[[御座船]][[天地丸]]の金色の鷲をもらいうけ献納した。古くから出世開運の神として尊崇を集めており、毎年12月の初酉の日から3日間の[[酉の市]]には福を願う多くの人々が訪れ大変な賑わいを見せる。
== 交通 == * JR[[成田線]][[安食駅]]徒歩10分
== 参考文献 == * 『ふるさとの文化遺産 郷土資料事典<12> 千葉県』人文社、1997年。
== 外部リンク == * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/5758/ 大鷲神社／千葉県公式観光情報サイト－まるごとe! ちば－]

[[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%A0%84%E7%94%BA)]] '''Ōwashi Shrine''' (大鷲神社)
* Another name for [[Ueno Province First Shrine]] * Another name for [[Shimotsuke Province First Shrine]]
{{Disambiguation}}
== Overview ==
'''Ōwashi Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ajiki]], [[Sakae Town]], [[Inba District]], [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan (formerly part of [[Shimousa Province]], [[Shimohabu District]]). The former shrine rank was "unranked shrine" under the modern shrine ranking system.
The shrine enshrines [[Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto]], [[Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]], and [[Yamato Takeru]].
== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unclear. It is said to be the site where [[Yamato Takeru]] established a temporary encampment and enshrined the ancestral deity during his eastern expedition. The current main hall, based on an inscription on its decorative metal fittings, was constructed in the second year of Tenpō (1831) and is designated as a tangible cultural property of Sakae Town.
During the [[Edo period]], the shrine received the devotion of [[Kasuga no Tsubone]], the wet nurse of [[Tokugawa Iemitsu]]. When Iemitsu was still known as Takechiyo, Kasuga no Tsubone prayed at this shrine for his future success. Upon becoming the third [[Shōgunate|shogun]], Iemitsu donated a golden eagle from his official vessel "Tenchimaru" to the shrine as an expression of gratitude. The shrine has long been revered as a deity of success and good fortune.
The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.
== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from [[Ajiki Station]] on the [[Narita Line]]
== References ==
* "Furusato no Bunka Isan Kyōdo Shiryō Jiten <12> Chiba-ken" (Cultural Heritage of Our Hometown, Local Materials Encyclopedia <12> Chiba Prefecture), Jinbunsha, 1997.
== External Links ==
* [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/5758/ Ōwashi Shrine / Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Information Site - Marugoto e! Chiba -]

The shrine has long been revered as a deity of success and good fortune.
The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.
== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from [[:en:Ajiki_Station|Ajiki Station]] on the [[:en:Narita_Line|Narita Line]]

revision.text:
[[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%A0%84%E7%94%BA)]] {{神社 |名称=大鷲神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Otori jinja sakae haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]| | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location=[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity=[[天日鷲神|天乃日鷲命]]・[[大国主|大巳貴命]]・[[スクナビコナ|小名彦命]]・[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival= |神事= }} '''Ōwashi Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ajiki|ja|安食 (栄町)}}, [[:en:Sakae,_Chiba|Sakae Town]], [[:en:Inba_District|Inba District]], [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan (formerly part of [[Shimousa Province]], [[:ja:下埴生郡|Shimohabu District]]). The former shrine rank was "unranked shrine" under the modern shrine ranking system.
The shrine enshrines [[Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto]], [[Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]], and [[Yamato Takeru]].
== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unclear. It is said to be the site where [[Yamato Takeru]] established a temporary encampment and enshrined the ancestral deity during his eastern expedition. The current main hall, based on an inscription on its decorative metal fittings, was constructed in the second year of Tenpō (1831) and is designated as a tangible cultural property of Sakae Town.
During the [[Edo period]], the shrine received the devotion of [[:en:Lady_Kasuga|Kasuga no Tsubone]], the wet nurse of [[Tokugawa Iemitsu]]. When Iemitsu was still known as Takechiyo, Kasuga no Tsubone prayed at this shrine for his future success. Upon becoming the third [[:en:Shogun|shogun]], Iemitsu donated a golden eagle from his official vessel "Tenchimaru" to the shrine as an expression of gratitude. The shrine has long been revered as a deity of success and good fortune.
The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.
== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from [[:en:Ajiki_Station|Ajiki Station]] on the [[:en:Narita_Line|Narita Line]]
== References ==
* "Furusato no Bunka Isan Kyōdo Shiryō Jiten <12> Chiba-ken" (Cultural Heritage of Our Hometown, Local Materials Encyclopedia <12> Chiba Prefecture), Jinbunsha, 1997.

[[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%A0%84%E7%94%BA)]] {{神社 |名称=大鷲神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Otori jinja sakae haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]| | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location=[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity=[[天日鷲神|天乃日鷲命]]・[[大国主|大巳貴命]]・[[スクナビコナ|小名彦命]]・[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival= |神事= }} '''Ōwashi Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ajiki|ja|安食 (栄町)}}, [[:en:Sakae,_Chiba|Sakae Town]], [[:en:Inba_District|Inba District]], [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan (formerly part of [[Shimousa Province]], [[:ja:下埴生郡|Shimohabu District]]). The former shrine rank was "unranked shrine" under the modern shrine ranking system.
The shrine enshrines [[Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto]], [[Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]], and [[Yamato Takeru]].
== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unclear. It is said to be the site where [[Yamato Takeru]] established a temporary encampment and enshrined the ancestral deity during his eastern expedition. The current main hall, based on an inscription on its decorative metal fittings, was constructed in the second year of Tenpō (1831) and is designated as a tangible cultural property of Sakae Town.
During the [[Edo period]], the shrine received the devotion of [[:en:Lady_Kasuga|Kasuga no Tsubone]], the wet nurse of [[Tokugawa Iemitsu]]. When Iemitsu was still known as Takechiyo, Kasuga no Tsubone prayed at this shrine for his future success. Upon becoming the third [[:en:Shogun|shogun]], Iemitsu donated a golden eagle from his official vessel "Tenchimaru" to the shrine as an expression of gratitude. The shrine has long been revered as a deity of success and good fortune.
The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.
== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from [[:en:Ajiki_Station|Ajiki Station]] on the [[:en:Narita_Line|Narita Line]]
== References ==
* "Furusato no Bunka Isan Kyōdo Shiryō Jiten <12> Chiba-ken" (Cultural Heritage of Our Hometown, Local Materials Encyclopedia <12> Chiba Prefecture), Jinbunsha, 1997.
== External Links ==
* [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/5758/ Ōwashi Shrine / Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Information Site - Marugoto e! Chiba -]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=大鷲神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Otori jinja sakae haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]| | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location=[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity=[[天日鷲神|天乃日鷲命]]・[[大国主|大巳貴命]]・[[スクナビコナ|小名彦命]]・[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival= |神事= }} '''Ōwashi Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ajiki|ja|安食 (栄町)|12=draft}}, [[:en:Sakae,_Chiba|Sakae Town]], [[:en:Inba_District|Inba District]], [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan (formerly part of [[Shimousa Province]], [[:ja:下埴生郡|Shimohabu District]]). The former shrine rank was "unranked shrine" under the modern shrine ranking system.
The shrine enshrines [[Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto]], [[Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]], and [[Yamato Takeru]].
== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unclear. It is said to be the site where [[Yamato Takeru]] established a temporary encampment and enshrined the ancestral deity during his eastern expedition. The current main hall, based on an inscription on its decorative metal fittings, was constructed in the second year of Tenpō (1831) and is designated as a tangible cultural property of Sakae Town.
During the [[Edo period]], the shrine received the devotion of [[:en:Lady_Kasuga|Kasuga no Tsubone]], the wet nurse of [[Tokugawa Iemitsu]]. When Iemitsu was still known as Takechiyo, Kasuga no Tsubone prayed at this shrine for his future success. Upon becoming the third [[:en:Shogun|shogun]], Iemitsu donated a golden eagle from his official vessel "Tenchimaru" to the shrine as an expression of gratitude. The shrine has long been revered as a deity of success and good fortune.
The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.
== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from [[:en:Ajiki_Station|Ajiki Station]] on the [[:en:Narita_Line|Narita Line]]
== References ==
* "Furusato no Bunka Isan Kyōdo Shiryō Jiten <12> Chiba-ken" (Cultural Heritage of Our Hometown, Local Materials Encyclopedia <12> Chiba Prefecture), Jinbunsha, 1997.
== External Links ==
* [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/5758/ Ōwashi Shrine / Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Information Site - Marugoto e! Chiba -]

{{神社 |名称=大鷲神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Otori jinja sakae haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]| | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location=[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity=[[天日鷲神|天乃日鷲命]]・[[大国主|大巳貴命]]・[[スクナビコナ|小名彦命]]・[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival= |神事= }} '''Ōwashi Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ajiki|ja|安食 (栄町)|12=draft}}, [[:en:Sakae,_Chiba|Sakae Town]], [[:en:Inba_District|Inba District]], [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan (formerly part of [[Shimousa Province]], [[:ja:下埴生郡|Shimohabu District]]). The former shrine rank was "unranked shrine" under the modern shrine ranking system.
The shrine enshrines [[Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto]], [[Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]], and [[Yamato Takeru]].
== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unclear. It is said to be the site where [[Yamato Takeru]] established a temporary encampment and enshrined the ancestral deity during his eastern expedition. The current main hall, based on an inscription on its decorative metal fittings, was constructed in the second year of Tenpō (1831) and is designated as a tangible cultural property of Sakae Town.
During the [[Edo period]], the shrine received the devotion of [[:en:Lady_Kasuga|Kasuga no Tsubone]], the wet nurse of [[Tokugawa Iemitsu]]. When Iemitsu was still known as Takechiyo, Kasuga no Tsubone prayed at this shrine for his future success. Upon becoming the third [[:en:Shogun|shogun]], Iemitsu donated a golden eagle from his official vessel "Tenchimaru" to the shrine as an expression of gratitude. The shrine has long been revered as a deity of success and good fortune.
The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.
== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from [[:en:Ajiki_Station|Ajiki Station]] on the [[:en:Narita_Line|Narita Line]]
== References ==
* "Furusato no Bunka Isan Kyōdo Shiryō Jiten <12> Chiba-ken" (Cultural Heritage of Our Hometown, Local Materials Encyclopedia <12> Chiba Prefecture), Jinbunsha, 1997.
== External Links ==
* [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/5758/ Ōwashi Shrine / Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Information Site - Marugoto e! Chiba -]

The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.
== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from [[:en:Ajiki_Station|Ajiki Station]] on the [[:en:Narita_Line|Narita Line]]
== References ==
* "Furusato no Bunka Isan Kyōdo Shiryō Jiten <12> Chiba-ken" (Cultural Heritage of Our Hometown, Local Materials Encyclopedia <12> Chiba Prefecture), Jinbunsha, 1997.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "rank" to "Rank"
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=大鷲神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Otori jinja sakae haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]| | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location=[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity=[[天日鷲神|天乃日鷲命]]・[[大国主|大巳貴命]]・[[スクナビコナ|小名彦命]]・[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival= |神事= }} '''Ōwashi Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ajiki|ja|安食 (栄町)|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[:en:Sakae,_Chiba|Sakae Town]], [[:en:Inba_District|Inba District]], [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan (formerly part of [[Shimousa Province]], [[:ja:下埴生郡|Shimohabu District]]). The former shrine Rank was "unRanked shrine" under the modern shrine Ranking system.
The shrine enshrines [[Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto]], [[Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]], and [[Yamato Takeru]].
== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unclear. It is said to be the site where [[Yamato Takeru]] established a temporary encampment and enshrined the ancestral deity during his eastern expedition. The current main hall, based on an inscription on its decorative metal fittings, was constructed in the second year of Tenpō (1831) and is designated as a tangible cultural property of Sakae Town.
During the [[Edo period]], the shrine received the devotion of [[:en:Lady_Kasuga|Kasuga no Tsubone]], the wet nurse of [[Tokugawa Iemitsu]]. When Iemitsu was still known as Takechiyo, Kasuga no Tsubone prayed at this shrine for his future success. Upon becoming the third [[:en:Shogun|shogun]], Iemitsu donated a golden eagle from his official vessel "Tenchimaru" to the shrine as an expression of gratitude. The shrine has long been revered as a deity of success and good fortune.
The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.
== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from [[:en:Ajiki_Station|Ajiki Station]] on the [[:en:Narita_Line|Narita Line]]
== References ==
* "Furusato no Bunka Isan Kyōdo Shiryō Jiten <12> Chiba-ken" (Cultural Heritage of Our Hometown, Local Materials Encyclopedia <12> Chiba Prefecture), Jinbunsha, 1997.
== External Links ==
* [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/5758/ Ōwashi Shrine / Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Information Site - Marugoto e! Chiba -]

revision.comment: 9 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大鷲神社 (栄町) up to rev 92223999
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=大鷲神社 |画像=[[File:Otori jinja sakae haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]| |所在地=[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} |祭神=[[天日鷲神|天乃日鷲命]]・[[大国主|大巳貴命]]・[[スクナビコナ|小名彦命]]・[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] |社格=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] |例祭= |神事= }} '''大鷲神社'''（おおわしじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]（[[下総国]][[下埴生郡]]）にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度#無格社|無格社]]。
[[天日鷲神|天乃日鷲命]]、[[大国主|大巳貴命]]、[[スクナビコナ|小名彦命]]、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]を祀る。
== 由緒 == 創祀の年代は明らかではないが、日本武尊が東夷征討の際、[[錦旗]]を立て仮の[[御野立所]]とし租神を奉斉した旧跡という。現存する本殿は凝宝珠金具の刻銘により[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）の造営とされ、栄町の[[有形文化財]]に指定されている。
[[江戸時代]]には[[徳川家光]]の[[乳母]][[春日局]]の崇敬を受けた。家光がまだ竹千代といっていたころ当社に竹千代の出世を祈願し、三代[[征夷大将軍|将軍]]になると報恩のため、将軍の[[御座船]][[天地丸]]の金色の鷲をもらいうけ献納した。古くから出世開運の神として尊崇を集めており、毎年12月の初酉の日から3日間の[[酉の市]]には福を願う多くの人々が訪れ大変な賑わいを見せる。
== 交通 == * JR[[成田線]][[安食駅]]徒歩10分
== 参考文献 == * 『ふるさとの文化遺産 郷土資料事典<12> 千葉県』人文社、1997年。
== 外部リンク == * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/5758/ 大鷲神社／千葉県公式観光情報サイト－まるごとe! ちば－]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおわししんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:栄町の建築物]] [[Category:栄町の歴史]] [[Category:千葉県の旧無格社]] [[カテゴリ:房総の魅力500選]]

== 由緒 == 創祀の年代は明らかではないが、日本武尊が東夷征討の際、[[錦旗]]を立て仮の[[御野立所]]とし租神を奉斉した旧跡という。現存する本殿は凝宝珠金具の刻銘により[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）の造営とされ、栄町の[[有形文化財]]に指定されている。

== 交通 == * JR[[成田線]][[安食駅]]徒歩10分
== 参考文献 == * 『ふるさとの文化遺産 郷土資料事典<12> 千葉県』人文社、1997年。

The founding date of the shrine is unclear. It is said to be the site where [[Yamato Takeru]] established a temporary encampment and enshrined the ancestral deity during his eastern expedition. The current main hall, based on an inscription on its decorative metal fittings, was constructed in the second year of Tenpō (1831) and is designated as a tangible cultural property of Sakae Town.
During the [[Edo period]], the shrine received the devotion of [[:en:Lady_Kasuga|Kasuga no Tsubone]], the wet nurse of [[Tokugawa Iemitsu]]. When Iemitsu was still known as Takechiyo, Kasuga no Tsubone prayed at this shrine for his future success. Upon becoming the third [[:en:Shogun|shogun]], Iemitsu donated a golden eagle from his official vessel "Tenchimaru" to the shrine as an expression of gratitude. The shrine has long been revered as a deity of success and good fortune.
The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.

[[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Sakae)]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%A0%84%E7%94%BA)]] [[ja:大鷽神社]] [[en:Ōtori Shrine (Sakae)]] [[ja:Ōtori Shrine (Sakae)]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Former unranked" to "Former Unranked"
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=大鷲神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Otori jinja sakae haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]| | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location=[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity=[[天日鷲神|天乃日鷲命]]・[[大国主|大巳貴命]]・[[スクナビコナ|小名彦命]]・[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival= |神事= }} '''Ōwashi Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ajiki|ja|安食 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[:en:Sakae,_Chiba|Sakae Town]], [[:en:Inba_District|Inba District]], [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan (formerly part of [[Shimousa Province]], [[:ja:下埴生郡|Shimohabu District]]). The former shrine Rank was "unranked shrine" under the modern shrine Ranking system.
The shrine enshrines [[Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto]], [[Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]], and [[Yamato Takeru]].
== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unclear. It is said to be the site where [[Yamato Takeru]] established a temporary encampment and enshrined the ancestral deity during his eastern expedition. The current main hall, based on an inscription on its decorative metal fittings, was constructed in the second year of Tenpō (1831) and is designated as a tangible cultural property of Sakae Town.
During the [[Edo period]], the shrine received the devotion of [[:en:Lady_Kasuga|Kasuga no Tsubone]], the wet nurse of [[Tokugawa Iemitsu]]. When Iemitsu was still known as Takechiyo, Kasuga no Tsubone prayed at this shrine for his future success. Upon becoming the third [[:en:Shogun|shogun]], Iemitsu donated a golden eagle from his official vessel "Tenchimaru" to the shrine as an expression of gratitude. The shrine has long been revered as a deity of success and good fortune.
The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.
== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from [[:en:Ajiki_Station|Ajiki Station]] on the [[:en:Narita_Line|Narita Line]]
== References ==
* "Furusato no Bunka Isan Kyōdo Shiryō Jiten <12> Chiba-ken" (Cultural Heritage of Our Hometown, Local Materials Encyclopedia <12> Chiba Prefecture), Jinbunsha, 1997.
== External Links ==
* [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/5758/ Ōwashi Shrine / Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Information Site - Marugoto e! Chiba -]

The current main hall, based on an inscription on its decorative metal fittings, was constructed in the second year of Tenpō (1831) and is designated as a tangible cultural property of Sakae Town.
During the [[Edo period]], the shrine received the devotion of [[:en:Lady_Kasuga|Kasuga no Tsubone]], the wet nurse of [[Tokugawa Iemitsu]]. When Iemitsu was still known as Takechiyo, Kasuga no Tsubone prayed at this shrine for his future success. Upon becoming the third [[:en:Shogun|shogun]], Iemitsu donated a golden eagle from his official vessel "Tenchimaru" to the shrine as an expression of gratitude. The shrine has long been revered as a deity of success and good fortune.
The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.
== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from [[:en:Ajiki_Station|Ajiki Station]] on the [[:en:Narita_Line|Narita Line]]
== References ==
* "Furusato no Bunka Isan Kyōdo Shiryō Jiten <12> Chiba-ken" (Cultural Heritage of Our Hometown, Local Materials Encyclopedia <12> Chiba Prefecture), Jinbunsha, 1997.

[[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Sakae)]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%A0%84%E7%94%BA)]] [[ja:大鷽神社]]
[[ja:Ōtori Shrine (Sakae)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:房総の魅力500選]] [[Category:栄町の建築物]] [[Category:栄町の歴史]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ja:大鷲神社 (栄町)]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=大鷲神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Otori jinja sakae haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]| | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location=[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity=[[天日鷲神|天乃日鷲命]]・[[大国主|大巳貴命]]・[[スクナビコナ|小名彦命]]・[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival= |神事= }} '''Ōwashi Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ajiki|ja|安食 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajiki|qq=}}, [[:en:Sakae,_Chiba|Sakae Town]], [[:en:Inba_District|Inba District]], [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan (formerly part of [[Shimousa Province]], [[:ja:下埴生郡|Shimohabu District]]). The former shrine Rank was "unranked shrine" under the modern shrine Ranking system.
The shrine enshrines [[Otori-sama|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto]], [[Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]], and [[Yamato Takeru]].
== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unclear. It is said to be the site where [[Yamato Takeru]] established a temporary encampment and enshrined the ancestral deity during his eastern expedition. The current main hall, based on an inscription on its decorative metal fittings, was constructed in the second year of Tenpō (1831) and is designated as a tangible cultural property of Sakae Town.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine received the devotion of [[:en:Lady_Kasuga|Kasuga no Tsubone]], the wet nurse of [[Tokugawa Iemitsu]]. When Iemitsu was still known as Takechiyo, Kasuga no Tsubone prayed at this shrine for his future success. Upon becoming the third [[:en:Shogun|shogun]], Iemitsu donated a golden eagle from his official vessel "Tenchimaru" to the shrine as an expression of gratitude. The shrine has long been revered as a deity of success and good fortune.
The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.
== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from [[:en:Ajiki_Station|Ajiki Station]] on the [[:en:Narita_Line|Narita Line]]
== References ==
* "Furusato no Bunka Isan Kyōdo Shiryō Jiten <12> Chiba-ken" (Cultural Heritage of Our Hometown, Local Materials Encyclopedia <12> Chiba Prefecture), Jinbunsha, 1997.
== External Links ==
* [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/5758/ Ōwashi Shrine / Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Information Site - Marugoto e! Chiba -]

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:栄の Municipal History]] into [[Category:Municipal History of Sakae (Chiba)]] via bot
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=大鷲神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Otori jinja sakae haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]| | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location=[[:en:千葉県|千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity=[[天日鷲神|天乃日鷲命]]・[[大国主|大巳貴命]]・[[スクナビコナ|小名彦命]]・[[:en:Yamato Takeru|日本武尊]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival= |神事= }} '''Ōwashi Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ajiki|ja|安食 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajiki|qq=}}, [[:en:Sakae,_Chiba|Sakae Town]], [[:en:Inba_District|Inba District]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan (formerly part of [[Shimousa Province]], [[:ja:下埴生郡|Shimohabu District]]). The former shrine Rank was "unranked shrine" under the modern shrine Ranking system.
The shrine enshrines [[Otori-sama|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto]], [[Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]], and [[Yamato Takeru]].
== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unclear. It is said to be the site where [[Yamato Takeru]] established a temporary encampment and enshrined the ancestral deity during his eastern expedition. The current main hall, based on an inscription on its decorative metal fittings, was constructed in the second year of Tenpō (1831) and is designated as a tangible cultural property of Sakae Town.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine received the devotion of [[:en:Lady_Kasuga|Kasuga no Tsubone]], the wet nurse of [[Tokugawa Iemitsu]]. When Iemitsu was still known as Takechiyo, Kasuga no Tsubone prayed at this shrine for his future success. Upon becoming the third [[:en:Shogun|shogun]], Iemitsu donated a golden eagle from his official vessel "Tenchimaru" to the shrine as an expression of gratitude. The shrine has long been revered as a deity of success and good fortune.
The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.
== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from [[:en:Ajiki_Station|Ajiki Station]] on the [[:en:Narita_Line|Narita Line]]
== References ==
* "Furusato no Bunka Isan Kyōdo Shiryō Jiten <12> Chiba-ken" (Cultural Heritage of Our Hometown, Local Materials Encyclopedia <12> Chiba Prefecture), Jinbunsha, 1997.
== External Links ==
* [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/5758/ Ōwashi Shrine / Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Information Site - Marugoto e! Chiba -]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "天日鷲神|" to "天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|"
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=大鷲神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Otori jinja sakae haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]| | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location=[[:en:千葉県|千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity=[[天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|天乃日鷲命]]・[[大国主|大巳貴命]]・[[スクナビコナ|小名彦命]]・[[:en:Yamato Takeru|日本武尊]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival= |神事= }} '''Ōwashi Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ajiki|ja|安食 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajiki|qq=}}, [[:en:Sakae,_Chiba|Sakae Town]], [[:en:Inba_District|Inba District]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan (formerly part of [[:en:Shimousa Province|Shimousa Province]], [[:ja:下埴生郡|Shimohabu District]]). The former shrine Rank was "unranked shrine" under the modern shrine Ranking system.
The shrine enshrines [[Otori-sama|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto]], [[Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]], and [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]].
== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unclear. It is said to be the site where [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] established a temporary encampment and enshrined the ancestral deity during his eastern expedition. The current main hall, based on an inscription on its decorative metal fittings, was constructed in the second year of Tenpō (1831) and is designated as a tangible cultural property of Sakae Town.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine received the devotion of [[:en:Lady_Kasuga|Kasuga no Tsubone]], the wet nurse of [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]]. When Iemitsu was still known as Takechiyo, Kasuga no Tsubone prayed at this shrine for his future success. Upon becoming the third [[:en:Shogun|shogun]], Iemitsu donated a golden eagle from his official vessel "Tenchimaru" to the shrine as an expression of gratitude. The shrine has long been revered as a deity of success and good fortune.
The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.
== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from [[:en:Ajiki_Station|Ajiki Station]] on the [[:en:Narita_Line|Narita Line]]
== References ==
* "Furusato no Bunka Isan Kyōdo Shiryō Jiten <12> Chiba-ken" (Cultural Heritage of Our Hometown, Local Materials Encyclopedia <12> Chiba Prefecture), Jinbunsha, 1997.
== External Links ==
* [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/5758/ Ōwashi Shrine / Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Information Site - Marugoto e! Chiba -]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "paes with no wikidata" to "Pages with no Wikidata"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=大鷲神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Otori jinja sakae haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]| | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location=[[:en:千葉県|千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity={{ill|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|天乃日鷲命|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|}}・[[大国主|大巳貴命]]・[[スクナビコナ|小名彦命]]・[[:en:Yamato Takeru|日本武尊]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival= |神事= }} '''Ōwashi Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ajiki|ja|安食 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajiki|qq=}}, [[:en:Sakae,_Chiba|Sakae Town]], [[:en:Inba_District|Inba District]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan (formerly part of [[:en:Shimousa Province|Shimousa Province]], [[:ja:下埴生郡|Shimohabu District]]). The former shrine Rank was "unranked shrine" under the modern shrine Ranking system.
The shrine enshrines [[Otori-sama|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto]], [[Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]], and [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]].
== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unclear. It is said to be the site where [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] established a temporary encampment and enshrined the ancestral deity during his eastern expedition. The current main hall, based on an inscription on its decorative metal fittings, was constructed in the second year of Tenpō (1831) and is designated as a tangible cultural property of Sakae Town.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine received the devotion of [[:en:Lady_Kasuga|Kasuga no Tsubone]], the wet nurse of [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]]. When Iemitsu was still known as Takechiyo, Kasuga no Tsubone prayed at this shrine for his future success. Upon becoming the third [[:en:Shogun|shogun]], Iemitsu donated a golden eagle from his official vessel "Tenchimaru" to the shrine as an expression of gratitude. The shrine has long been revered as a deity of success and good fortune.
The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.
== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from [[:en:Ajiki_Station|Ajiki Station]] on the [[:en:Narita_Line|Narita Line]]
== References ==
* "Furusato no Bunka Isan Kyōdo Shiryō Jiten <12> Chiba-ken" (Cultural Heritage of Our Hometown, Local Materials Encyclopedia <12> Chiba Prefecture), Jinbunsha, 1997.
== External Links ==
* [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/5758/ Ōwashi Shrine / Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Information Site - Marugoto e! Chiba -]
{{Shinto-stub}}
{{Ōtori Faith}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称=大鷲神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Otori jinja sakae haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]| | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location=[[:en:千葉県|千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity={{ill|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|天乃日鷲命|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|}}・[[大国主|大巳貴命]]・[[スクナビコナ|小名彦命]]・[[:en:Yamato Takeru|日本武尊]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival= |神事= }} '''Ōwashi Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ajiki|ja|安食 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajiki|qq=}}, [[:en:Sakae,_Chiba|Sakae Town]], [[:en:Inba_District|Inba District]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan (formerly part of [[:en:Shimousa Province|Shimousa Province]], [[:ja:下埴生郡|Shimohabu District]]). The former shrine Rank was "unranked shrine" under the modern shrine Ranking system.
The shrine enshrines [[Otori-sama|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto]], [[Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]], and [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]].
== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unclear. It is said to be the site where [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] established a temporary encampment and enshrined the ancestral deity during his eastern expedition. The current main hall, based on an inscription on its decorative metal fittings, was constructed in the second year of Tenpō (1831) and is designated as a tangible cultural property of Sakae Town.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine received the devotion of [[:en:Lady_Kasuga|Kasuga no Tsubone]], the wet nurse of [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]]. When Iemitsu was still known as Takechiyo, Kasuga no Tsubone prayed at this shrine for his future success. Upon becoming the third [[:en:Shogun|shogun]], Iemitsu donated a golden eagle from his official vessel "Tenchimaru" to the shrine as an expression of gratitude. The shrine has long been revered as a deity of success and good fortune.
The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.
== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from [[:en:Ajiki_Station|Ajiki Station]] on the [[:en:Narita_Line|Narita Line]]
== References ==
* "Furusato no Bunka Isan Kyōdo Shiryō Jiten <12> Chiba-ken" (Cultural Heritage of Our Hometown, Local Materials Encyclopedia <12> Chiba Prefecture), Jinbunsha, 1997.
== External Links ==
* [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/5758/ Ōwashi Shrine / Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Information Site - Marugoto e! Chiba -]

[[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Sakae)]] [[sn:E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%A0%84%E7%94%BA)]] [[ja:大鷽神社]]
[[ja:Ōtori Shrine (Sakae)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Top 500 Attractions of Bōsō Peninsula]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakae Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sakae (Chiba)]] [[en:Ōtori Shrine (Sakae)]]<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ja:大鷲神社 (栄町)]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]][[ja:大鷲神社 (栄町)]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称=大鷲神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Otori jinja sakae haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]| |location=[[:en:千葉県|千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} |deity={{ill|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|天乃日鷲命|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|}}・[[Ōkuninushi]]・[[Sukunabikona]]・[[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] |type=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] |festival= |神事= }} '''Ōwashi Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ajiki|ja|安食 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajiki|qq=|1=Ajiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17191282}}, [[:en:Sakae,_Chiba|Sakae Town]], [[:en:Inba_District|Inba District]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan (formerly part of [[:en:Shimousa Province|Shimousa Province]], [[:ja:下埴生郡|Shimohabu District]]). The former shrine Rank was "unranked shrine" under the modern shrine Ranking system.
The shrine enshrines [[Otori-sama|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto]], [[Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]], and [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]].
== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unclear. It is said to be the site where [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] established a temporary encampment and enshrined the ancestral deity during his eastern expedition. The current main hall, based on an inscription on its decorative metal fittings, was constructed in the second year of Tenpō (1831) and is designated as a tangible cultural property of Sakae Town.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine received the devotion of [[:en:Lady_Kasuga|Kasuga no Tsubone]], the wet nurse of [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]]. When Iemitsu was still known as Takechiyo, Kasuga no Tsubone prayed at this shrine for his future success. Upon becoming the third [[:en:Shogun|shogun]], Iemitsu donated a golden eagle from his official vessel "Tenchimaru" to the shrine as an expression of gratitude. The shrine has long been revered as a deity of success and good fortune.
The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.
== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from [[:en:Ajiki_Station|Ajiki Station]] on the [[:en:Narita_Line|Narita Line]]
== References ==
* "Furusato no Bunka Isan Kyōdo Shiryō Jiten <12> Chiba-ken" (Cultural Heritage of Our Hometown, Local Materials Encyclopedia <12> Chiba Prefecture), Jinbunsha, 1997.
== External Links ==
* [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/5758/ Ōwashi Shrine / Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Information Site - Marugoto e! Chiba -]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[ja:大鷲神社 (栄町)]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称=大鷲神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Otori jinja sakae haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]| |location=[[:en:千葉県|千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} |deity={{ill|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|天乃日鷲命|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|}}・[[Ōkuninushi]]・[[Sukunabikona]]・[[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] |type=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] |festival= |神事= }} '''Ōwashi Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ajiki|ja|安食 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajiki|qq=|1=Ajiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17191282}}, [[:en:Sakae,_Chiba|Sakae Town]], [[:en:Inba_District|Inba District]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan (formerly part of [[:en:Shimousa Province|Shimousa Province]], [[:ja:下埴生郡|Shimohabu District]]). The former shrine Rank was "unranked shrine" under the modern shrine Ranking system.
The shrine enshrines [[Otori-sama|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto]], [[Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]], and [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]].
== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unclear. It is said to be the site where [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] established a temporary encampment and enshrined the ancestral deity during his eastern expedition. The current main hall, based on an inscription on its decorative metal fittings, was constructed in the second year of Tenpō (1831) and is designated as a tangible cultural property of Sakae Town.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine received the devotion of [[:en:Lady_Kasuga|Kasuga no Tsubone]], the wet nurse of [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]]. When Iemitsu was still known as Takechiyo, Kasuga no Tsubone prayed at this shrine for his future success. Upon becoming the third [[:en:Shogun|shogun]], Iemitsu donated a golden eagle from his official vessel "Tenchimaru" to the shrine as an expression of gratitude. The shrine has long been revered as a deity of success and good fortune.
The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.
== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from [[:en:Ajiki_Station|Ajiki Station]] on the [[:en:Narita_Line|Narita Line]]
== References ==
* "Furusato no Bunka Isan Kyōdo Shiryō Jiten <12> Chiba-ken" (Cultural Heritage of Our Hometown, Local Materials Encyclopedia <12> Chiba Prefecture), Jinbunsha, 1997.
== External Links ==
* [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/5758/ Ōwashi Shrine / Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Information Site - Marugoto e! Chiba -]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大鷲神社 (栄町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[ja:大鷲神社 (栄町)]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称=大鷲神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Otori jinja sakae haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]| |location=[[:en:千葉県|千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} |deity={{ill|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|天乃日鷲命|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|}}・[[Ōkuninushi]]・[[Sukunabikona]]・[[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] |type=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] |festival= |神事= }} '''Ōwashi Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ajiki|ja|安食 (栄町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajiki|1=Ajiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17191282}}, [[:en:Sakae,_Chiba|Sakae Town]], [[:en:Inba_District|Inba District]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan (formerly part of [[:en:Shimousa Province|Shimousa Province]], [[:ja:下埴生郡|Shimohabu District]]). The former shrine Rank was "unranked shrine" under the modern shrine Ranking system.
The shrine enshrines [[Otori-sama|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto]], [[Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]], and [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]].
== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unclear. It is said to be the site where [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] established a temporary encampment and enshrined the ancestral deity during his eastern expedition. The current main hall, based on an inscription on its decorative metal fittings, was constructed in the second year of Tenpō (1831) and is designated as a tangible cultural property of Sakae Town.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine received the devotion of [[:en:Lady_Kasuga|Kasuga no Tsubone]], the wet nurse of [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]]. When Iemitsu was still known as Takechiyo, Kasuga no Tsubone prayed at this shrine for his future success. Upon becoming the third [[:en:Shogun|shogun]], Iemitsu donated a golden eagle from his official vessel "Tenchimaru" to the shrine as an expression of gratitude. The shrine has long been revered as a deity of success and good fortune.
The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.
== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from [[:en:Ajiki_Station|Ajiki Station]] on the [[:en:Narita_Line|Narita Line]]
== References ==
* "Furusato no Bunka Isan Kyōdo Shiryō Jiten <12> Chiba-ken" (Cultural Heritage of Our Hometown, Local Materials Encyclopedia <12> Chiba Prefecture), Jinbunsha, 1997.
== External Links ==
* [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/5758/ Ōwashi Shrine / Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Information Site - Marugoto e! Chiba -]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大鷲神社 (栄町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[ja:大鷲神社 (栄町)]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称=大鷲神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Otori jinja sakae haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]| |location=[[:en:千葉県|千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} |deity={{ill|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|天乃日鷲命|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|}}・[[Ōkuninushi]]・[[Sukunabikona]]・[[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] |type=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] |festival= |神事= }} '''Ōwashi Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ajiki|ja|安食 (栄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajiki|1=Ajiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17191282}}, [[:en:Sakae,_Chiba|Sakae Town]], [[:en:Inba_District|Inba District]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan (formerly part of [[:en:Shimousa Province|Shimousa Province]], [[:ja:下埴生郡|Shimohabu District]]). The former shrine Rank was "unranked shrine" under the modern shrine Ranking system.
The shrine enshrines [[Otori-sama|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto]], [[Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]], and [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]].
== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unclear. It is said to be the site where [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] established a temporary encampment and enshrined the ancestral deity during his eastern expedition. The current main hall, based on an inscription on its decorative metal fittings, was constructed in the second year of Tenpō (1831) and is designated as a tangible cultural property of Sakae Town.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine received the devotion of [[:en:Lady_Kasuga|Kasuga no Tsubone]], the wet nurse of [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]]. When Iemitsu was still known as Takechiyo, Kasuga no Tsubone prayed at this shrine for his future success. Upon becoming the third [[:en:Shogun|shogun]], Iemitsu donated a golden eagle from his official vessel "Tenchimaru" to the shrine as an expression of gratitude. The shrine has long been revered as a deity of success and good fortune.
The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.
== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from [[:en:Ajiki_Station|Ajiki Station]] on the [[:en:Narita_Line|Narita Line]]
== References ==
* "Furusato no Bunka Isan Kyōdo Shiryō Jiten <12> Chiba-ken" (Cultural Heritage of Our Hometown, Local Materials Encyclopedia <12> Chiba Prefecture), Jinbunsha, 1997.
== External Links ==
* [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/5758/ Ōwashi Shrine / Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Information Site - Marugoto e! Chiba -]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大鷲神社 (栄町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[ja:大鷲神社 (栄町)]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称=大鷲神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Otori jinja sakae haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]| |location=[[:en:千葉県|千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} |deity={{ill|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|天乃日鷲命|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|}}・[[Ōkuninushi]]・[[Sukunabikona]]・[[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] |type=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] |festival= |神事= }} '''Ōwashi Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ajiki|ja|安食 (栄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajiki|1=Ajiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17191282}}, [[:en:Sakae,_Chiba|Sakae Town]], [[:en:Inba_District|Inba District]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan (formerly part of [[:en:Shimousa Province|Shimousa Province]], [[:ja:下埴生郡|Shimohabu District]]). The former shrine Rank was "unranked shrine" under the modern shrine Ranking system.
The shrine enshrines [[Otori-sama|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto]], [[Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]], and [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]].
== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unclear. It is said to be the site where [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] established a temporary encampment and enshrined the ancestral deity during his eastern expedition. The current main hall, based on an inscription on its decorative metal fittings, was constructed in the second year of Tenpō (1831) and is designated as a tangible cultural property of Sakae Town.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine received the devotion of [[:en:Lady_Kasuga|Kasuga no Tsubone]], the wet nurse of [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]]. When Iemitsu was still known as Takechiyo, Kasuga no Tsubone prayed at this shrine for his future success. Upon becoming the third [[:en:Shogun|shogun]], Iemitsu donated a golden eagle from his official vessel "Tenchimaru" to the shrine as an expression of gratitude. The shrine has long been revered as a deity of success and good fortune.
The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.
== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from [[:en:Ajiki_Station|Ajiki Station]] on the [[:en:Narita_Line|Narita Line]]
== References ==
* "Furusato no Bunka Isan Kyōdo Shiryō Jiten <12> Chiba-ken" (Cultural Heritage of Our Hometown, Local Materials Encyclopedia <12> Chiba Prefecture), Jinbunsha, 1997.
== External Links ==
* [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/5758/ Ōwashi Shrine / Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Information Site - Marugoto e! Chiba -]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大鷲神社 (栄町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大鷲神社 (栄町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大鷲神社 (栄町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[ja:大鷲神社 (栄町)]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称=大鷲神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Otori jinja sakae haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]| |location=[[:en:千葉県|千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} |deity={{ill|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|天乃日鷲命|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|}}・[[Ōkuninushi]]・[[Sukunabikona]]・[[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] |type=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] |festival= |神事= }} '''Ōwashi Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ajiki|ja|安食 (栄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajiki|1=Ajiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17191282}}, [[:en:Sakae,_Chiba|Sakae Town]], [[:en:Inba_District|Inba District]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan (formerly part of [[:en:Shimousa Province|Shimousa Province]], [[:ja:下埴生郡|Shimohabu District]]). The former shrine Rank was "unranked shrine" under the modern shrine Ranking system.
The shrine enshrines [[Otori-sama|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto]], [[Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]], and [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]].
== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unclear. It is said to be the site where [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] established a temporary encampment and enshrined the ancestral deity during his eastern expedition. The current main hall, based on an inscription on its decorative metal fittings, was constructed in the second year of Tenpō (1831) and is designated as a tangible cultural property of Sakae Town.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine received the devotion of [[:en:Lady_Kasuga|Kasuga no Tsubone]], the wet nurse of [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]]. When Iemitsu was still known as Takechiyo, Kasuga no Tsubone prayed at this shrine for his future success. Upon becoming the third [[:en:Shogun|shogun]], Iemitsu donated a golden eagle from his official vessel "Tenchimaru" to the shrine as an expression of gratitude. The shrine has long been revered as a deity of success and good fortune.
The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.
== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from [[:en:Ajiki_Station|Ajiki Station]] on the [[:en:Narita_Line|Narita Line]]
== References ==
* "Furusato no Bunka Isan Kyōdo Shiryō Jiten <12> Chiba-ken" (Cultural Heritage of Our Hometown, Local Materials Encyclopedia <12> Chiba Prefecture), Jinbunsha, 1997.
== External Links ==
* [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/5758/ Ōwashi Shrine / Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Information Site - Marugoto e! Chiba -]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大鷲神社 (栄町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大鷲神社 (栄町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大鷲神社 (栄町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[ja:大鷲神社 (栄町)]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称=大鷲神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Otori jinja sakae haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]| |location=[[:en:千葉県|千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} |deity={{ill|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|天乃日鷲命|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|}}・[[Ōkuninushi]]・[[Sukunabikona]]・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}} |type=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] |festival= |神事= }} '''Ōwashi Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ajiki|ja|安食 (栄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajiki|1=Ajiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17191282}}, {{ill|Sakae,_Chiba|en|Sakae,_Chiba|WD=Q1202754|lt=Sakae Town}}, {{ill|Inba_District|en|Inba_District|WD=Q677245|lt=Inba District}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan (formerly part of [[:en:Shimousa Province|Shimousa Province]], [[:ja:下埴生郡|Shimohabu District]]). The former shrine Rank was "unranked shrine" under the modern shrine Ranking system.
The shrine enshrines [[Otori-sama|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto]], [[Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]], and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}}.
== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unclear. It is said to be the site where {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}} established a temporary encampment and enshrined the ancestral deity during his eastern expedition. The current main hall, based on an inscription on its decorative metal fittings, was constructed in the second year of Tenpō (1831) and is designated as a tangible cultural property of Sakae Town.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine received the devotion of {{ill|Lady_Kasuga|en|Lady_Kasuga|WD=Q835747|lt=Kasuga no Tsubone}}, the wet nurse of {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|en|Tokugawa Iemitsu|WD=Q314481|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu}}. When Iemitsu was still known as Takechiyo, Kasuga no Tsubone prayed at this shrine for his future success. Upon becoming the third {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=shogun}}, Iemitsu donated a golden eagle from his official vessel "Tenchimaru" to the shrine as an expression of gratitude. The shrine has long been revered as a deity of success and good fortune.
The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.
== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from {{ill|Ajiki_Station|en|Ajiki_Station|WD=Q864215|lt=Ajiki Station}} on the {{ill|Narita_Line|en|Narita_Line|WD=Q1070027|lt=Narita Line}}
== References ==
* "Furusato no Bunka Isan Kyōdo Shiryō Jiten <12> Chiba-ken" (Cultural Heritage of Our Hometown, Local Materials Encyclopedia <12> Chiba Prefecture), Jinbunsha, 1997.
== External Links ==
* [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/5758/ Ōwashi Shrine / Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Information Site - Marugoto e! Chiba -]

{{Infobox religious building |名称=大鷲神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Otori jinja sakae haiden.JPG|250px|拝殿]]| |location=[[:en:千葉県|千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]][[安食 (栄町)|安食]]3620 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|50|26|N|140|14|17|E|scale:30000}} |deity={{ill|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|天乃日鷲命|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|}}・[[Ōkuninushi]]・[[Sukunabikona]]・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} |type=無格社 |創建= |別名= |本殿=[[権現造]] |festival= |神事= }} '''Ōwashi Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ajiki|ja|安食 (栄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajiki|1=Ajiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17191282}}, {{ill|Sakae,_Chiba|en|Sakae,_Chiba|WD=Q1202754|lt=Sakae Town|1=Sakae, Chiba}}, {{ill|Inba_District|en|Inba_District|WD=Q677245|lt=Inba District|1=Inba District}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan (formerly part of [[:en:Shimousa Province|Shimousa Province]], [[:ja:下埴生郡|Shimohabu District]]). The former shrine Rank was "unranked shrine" under the modern shrine Ranking system.
The shrine enshrines [[Otori-sama|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto]], [[Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]], and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}.
== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unclear. It is said to be the site where {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} established a temporary encampment and enshrined the ancestral deity during his eastern expedition. The current main hall, based on an inscription on its decorative metal fittings, was constructed in the second year of Tenpō (1831) and is designated as a tangible cultural property of Sakae Town.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine received the devotion of {{ill|Lady_Kasuga|en|Lady_Kasuga|WD=Q835747|lt=Kasuga no Tsubone|1=Lady Kasuga}}, the wet nurse of {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|en|Tokugawa Iemitsu|WD=Q314481|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|1=Tokugawa Iemitsu}}. When Iemitsu was still known as Takechiyo, Kasuga no Tsubone prayed at this shrine for his future success. Upon becoming the third {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=shogun|1=Shogun}}, Iemitsu donated a golden eagle from his official vessel "Tenchimaru" to the shrine as an expression of gratitude. The shrine has long been revered as a deity of success and good fortune.
The annual "Tori no Ichi" festival, held for three days starting from the first Tori day of December, attracts many visitors praying for prosperity, creating a lively atmosphere.
== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from {{ill|Ajiki_Station|en|Ajiki_Station|WD=Q864215|lt=Ajiki Station|1=Ajiki Station}} on the {{ill|Narita_Line|en|Narita_Line|WD=Q1070027|lt=Narita Line|1=Narita Line}}
== References ==
* "Furusato no Bunka Isan Kyōdo Shiryō Jiten <12> Chiba-ken" (Cultural Heritage of Our Hometown, Local Materials Encyclopedia <12> Chiba Prefecture), Jinbunsha, 1997.
== External Links ==
* [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/5758/ Ōwashi Shrine / Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Information Site - Marugoto e! Chiba -]
